name: Mazen Amr
title: Front-End Developer
tagline: Building the future, one line of code at a time.
about: I am a front-end developer and Computer Science student at Lancaster University Leipzig with a passion for building clean, immersive, and purposeful web experiences. This portfolio represents my evolution from writing static HTML by hand in Phase 1 to architecting a fully dynamic, AI-assisted application in Phase 2 — loading all content from JSON, animating with JavaScript, and documenting the entire process in LaTeX. I believe great development is about more than working code — it is about craft, curiosity, and continuous learning.

experience.title: Front-End Developer
experience.description: Designed and built responsive, accessible web applications entirely from scratch using semantic HTML5, modern CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript. Managed full project lifecycle from design to deployment using Git and GitHub.

experience.title: AI-Assisted Developer
experience.description: Explored and documented AI-assisted development workflows using Claude and GitHub Copilot. Learned to critically evaluate AI output, integrate it with hand-written code, and reflect on the experience through structured LaTeX reports.

projects.title: CV Portfolio — Phase 1
projects.description: A fully handcrafted professional portfolio built from scratch with zero frameworks, zero libraries, and zero AI assistance. Features semantic HTML, custom CSS animations, vanilla JavaScript interactivity, a Bash analysis script, and a clean Git branching workflow. Scored 90% as a solo submission.

projects.title: Dynamic CV Portfolio — Phase 2
projects.description: An AI-assisted rebuild of Phase 1 featuring a fully dynamic data layer driven by JSON, a particle universe background, cycling typewriter hero, 3D card tilt effects, animated skill bars, magnetic buttons, custom dual cursor, scroll-triggered reveals, and page transition overlays. Documented in a LaTeX reflection report.

achievements: Scored 90% on Assessment A as a solo submission
Competed individually against group teams of up to three members
Implemented full Git branching and pull request workflow independently
Built a dynamic JSON-driven web application with immersive visual effects
Documented AI-assisted development process in a structured LaTeX report
